differentials.name: renal colic = acute severe spasmodic / steady continuous flank pain
differentials.raw: renal colic = acute severe spasmodic / steady continuous flank pain

differentials.name: frequently radiating into pelvis / groin / scrotum / labia
differentials.raw: frequently radiating into pelvis / groin / scrotum / labia

differentials.name: hematuria (85%): absent in completely obstructing stone
differentials.raw: hematuria (85%): absent in completely obstructing stone

differentials.name: Medical expulsive therapy: α-blocker, calcium channel blocker, steroids,
differentials.raw: Medical expulsive therapy: α-blocker, calcium channel blocker, steroids,

differentials.name: Hydration (during strenuous physical activity, at bedtime, within 3 hours after meal) maintaining urine output of 2-3 L/days
differentials.raw: Hydration (during strenuous physical activity, at bedtime, within 3 hours after meal) maintaining urine output of 2-3 L/days

differentials.name: Diet: restrict amounts of protein, sodium, calcium
differentials.raw: Diet: restrict amounts of protein, sodium, calcium

differentials.name: Drugs: thiazide diuretics
differentials.raw: Drugs: thiazide diuretics (lowers urinary calcium), allopurinol (lowers urate + oxalate excretion)

differentials.name: Spontaneous passage of ureteral calculi (in 93%) ◊  Stones > 6 mm rarely pass!
differentials.raw: Spontaneous passage of ureteral calculi (in 93%) ◊  Stones > 6 mm rarely pass!

differentials.name: Without treatment stone recurrence is 10% at 1 year, 33% at 5 years, 50% at 10 years, 75% by 20 years
differentials.raw: Without treatment stone recurrence is 10% at 1 year, 33% at 5 years, 50% at 10 years, 75% by 20 years

context: =  URETEROLITHIASIS Site:     at points of ureteral narrowing (a)  ureterovesical junction (UVJ) (b)  ureteropelvic junction (UPJ) (c)  iliac vessel crossing Plain radiography = KUB (45% sensitive, 77% specific): Useful:   planning ESWL, monitoring stone fragments in 1-2-week intervals Visualization: ◊  90% of urinary calculi are radiopaque Confounding factors: small size of calculus, overlying bowel gas / fecal matter, osseous structures (transverse process, sacrum), extrarenal calcifications (gallstone, calcific pancreatitis, mesenteric lymph node, arterial calcification, phlebolith), obesit
